meta.name: LinguaLoop Grammar Knowledge Base
meta.description: Curated, ordered grammar spine from A1 to B2. Explanations are authored (never generated). The lesson generator uses 'structure_hint' to build dialogues; the app shows 'explanation'/'vi_note'/'examples' in the Notice panel and uses 'frames' for speaking practice. The B2 block (orders 54+) covers the higher-range structures that IELTS rewards.

points.title: The verb 'to be' (am / is / are)
points.explanation: Use 'am' with I, 'is' with he/she/it, and 'are' with you/we/they.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'am' với I, 'is' với he/she/it, 'are' với you/we/they.

points.title: Subject pronouns & possessive adjectives
points.explanation: Subject pronouns do the action (I, you, he...). Possessive adjectives show owning (my, your, his...).
points.vi_note: Đại từ chủ ngữ (I, you, he...) và tính từ sở hữu (my, your, his...).

points.title: Articles a / an
points.structure_hint: a + consonant sound; an + vowel sound
points.explanation: Use 'a' before a consonant sound and 'an' before a vowel sound, for one singular thing.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'a' trước phụ âm, 'an' trước nguyên âm, cho một danh từ số ít.

points.title: Plural nouns (regular -s)
points.explanation: Add -s to make most nouns plural. Add -es after s, x, ch, sh.
points.vi_note: Thêm -s để tạo số nhiều; thêm -es sau s, x, ch, sh.

points.title: this / that / these / those
points.explanation: Use this/these for things near you, that/those for things far from you.
points.vi_note: this/these cho vật ở gần, that/those cho vật ở xa.

points.title: Present simple — affirmative
points.explanation: Use the present simple for habits and facts. Add -s for he/she/it.
points.vi_note: Hiện tại đơn cho thói quen và sự thật. Thêm -s cho he/she/it.

points.title: Present simple — negatives & questions (do / does)
points.explanation: Use don't/doesn't for negatives and Do/Does to ask questions. The main verb stays in base form.
points.vi_note: Dùng don't/doesn't cho phủ định và Do/Does để hỏi; động từ chính giữ nguyên.

points.title: have / have got
points.explanation: Use 'have' or 'have got' to talk about things you own or relationships.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'have'/'have got' để nói về sở hữu hoặc quan hệ.

points.title: there is / there are
points.structure_hint: There is + singular; There are + plural
points.explanation: Use 'there is' for one thing and 'there are' for more than one to say something exists.
points.vi_note: 'there is' cho một vật, 'there are' cho nhiều vật, để nói có cái gì đó.
examples: There is a gym near my house.

points.title: Adjectives + word order
points.explanation: Put the adjective before the noun. Adjectives do not change for plural.
points.vi_note: Đặt tính từ trước danh từ; tính từ không đổi khi số nhiều.

points.title: can / can't (ability)
points.explanation: Use 'can' to say you are able to do something and 'can't' for not able.
points.vi_note: 'can' = có thể, 'can't' = không thể, + động từ nguyên mẫu.

points.title: Prepositions of place
points.structure_hint: in / on / under / next to / behind + noun
points.explanation: These words show where something is.
points.vi_note: Các từ chỉ vị trí: in (trong), on (trên), under (dưới), next to (cạnh), behind (sau).
examples: The ball is under the chair.
My phone is on the table.
The gym is next to the shop.

points.title: Prepositions of time (at / in / on)
points.structure_hint: at + time; on + day; in + month/year
points.explanation: Use 'at' for clock times, 'on' for days, 'in' for months and years.
points.vi_note: 'at' cho giờ, 'on' cho ngày, 'in' cho tháng/năm.

points.title: Object pronouns (me, him, her, us, them)
points.explanation: Object pronouns receive the action: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.
points.vi_note: Đại từ tân ngữ nhận hành động: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.

points.title: Present continuous (am/is/are + -ing)
points.explanation: Use the present continuous for actions happening now.
points.vi_note: Hiện tại tiếp diễn cho hành động đang xảy ra bây giờ.

points.title: like / love / hate + -ing
points.explanation: After like, love, and hate, use the -ing form of the verb to talk about activities.
points.vi_note: Sau like/love/hate, dùng động từ thêm -ing để nói về hoạt động.

points.title: Adverbs of frequency
points.explanation: These words say how often. They usually go before the main verb.
points.vi_note: Trạng từ tần suất (always, usually, sometimes, never) thường đứng trước động từ chính.
examples: I always train in the morning.

points.title: Imperatives
points.structure_hint: base verb (+ object); Don't + base verb
points.explanation: Use the base verb to give instructions or orders. Use 'Don't' to tell someone not to do something.
points.vi_note: Dùng động từ nguyên mẫu để ra lệnh/hướng dẫn; 'Don't' để bảo đừng làm.

points.title: some / any
points.explanation: Use 'some' in positive sentences and 'any' in negatives and questions.
points.vi_note: 'some' trong câu khẳng định; 'any' trong câu phủ định và câu hỏi.

points.title: Past simple — to be (was / were)
points.explanation: Use 'was' and 'were' to talk about states in the past.
points.vi_note: 'was' cho I/he/she/it, 'were' cho you/we/they, nói về quá khứ.

points.title: Past simple — regular verbs (-ed)
points.explanation: To talk about finished actions in the past, add -ed to most verbs.
points.vi_note: Thêm -ed vào động từ để nói về hành động đã xong trong quá khứ.

points.title: Past simple — irregular verbs
points.explanation: Many common verbs have special past forms you must learn: go->went, have->had, see->saw, eat->ate.
points.vi_note: Nhiều động từ thông dụng có dạng quá khứ bất quy tắc: go->went, have->had, see->saw.

points.title: Past simple — negatives & questions (didn't / did)
points.structure_hint: didn't + base verb; Did + subject + base verb?
points.explanation: Use 'didn't' for negatives and 'Did' for questions. The main verb returns to base form.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'didn't' cho phủ định và 'Did' để hỏi; động từ chính về nguyên mẫu.

points.title: Comparative adjectives
points.structure_hint: short adj + -er + than; more + long adj + than
points.explanation: Add -er to short adjectives and use 'more' before long ones to compare two things, with 'than'.
points.vi_note: Tính từ ngắn + -er, tính từ dài dùng 'more', + 'than' để so sánh hai vật.
examples: This phone is faster than mine.
Football is more exciting than golf.

points.title: Superlative adjectives
points.structure_hint: the + adj-est; the most + long adj
points.explanation: Use 'the ...-est' or 'the most ...' to say something is number one in a group.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'the ...-est' hoặc 'the most ...' để nói cái nhất trong nhóm.
examples: It was the most exciting game.

points.title: be going to (future plans)
points.explanation: Use 'be going to' for plans and intentions in the future.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'be going to' cho kế hoạch và dự định tương lai.
examples: I am going to train tomorrow.
We are going to watch the final.
She is going to buy a phone.

points.title: will (predictions & quick decisions)
points.explanation: Use 'will' for predictions and decisions made at the moment of speaking.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'will' cho dự đoán và quyết định tức thời.

points.title: have to / must (obligation)
points.explanation: Use 'have to' and 'must' to say something is necessary.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'have to'/'must' để nói điều gì đó là bắt buộc/cần thiết.

points.title: should / shouldn't (advice)
points.explanation: Use 'should' to give advice and 'shouldn't' to advise against something.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'should' để khuyên nên, 'shouldn't' để khuyên không nên.

points.title: Countable & uncountable; how much / how many
points.structure_hint: How many + countable plural; How much + uncountable
points.explanation: Use 'how many' with things you can count and 'how much' with things you cannot count.
points.vi_note: 'how many' với danh từ đếm được, 'how much' với danh từ không đếm được.
examples: How many shoes do you have?

points.title: Quantifiers: a lot of / a few / a little
points.structure_hint: a lot of (both); a few + countable; a little + uncountable
points.explanation: Use 'a few' with countable nouns, 'a little' with uncountable nouns, and 'a lot of' with both.
points.vi_note: 'a few' với đếm được, 'a little' với không đếm được, 'a lot of' với cả hai.
examples: I have a lot of friends.

points.title: Possessive pronouns (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs)
points.explanation: Possessive pronouns replace 'possessive adjective + noun' (my phone -> mine).
points.vi_note: Đại từ sở hữu thay cho 'tính từ sở hữu + danh từ' (my phone -> mine).

points.title: Connectors: and, but, because, so
points.explanation: Join ideas: 'and' adds, 'but' contrasts, 'because' gives a reason, 'so' gives a result.
points.vi_note: Nối ý: 'and' thêm, 'but' tương phản, 'because' lý do, 'so' kết quả.
examples: I trained because I want to be fit.
I was tired, so I slept.

points.title: Adverbs of manner (quickly, carefully)
points.explanation: Adverbs of manner say how an action is done. Many are made by adding -ly to an adjective.
points.vi_note: Trạng từ chỉ cách thức (thường = tính từ + -ly) nói hành động được làm thế nào.

points.title: Present perfect — introduction (ever / never)
points.explanation: Use the present perfect for life experiences without a specific time. Use 'ever' in questions, 'never' for none.
points.vi_note: Hiện tại hoàn thành cho trải nghiệm không nói thời gian cụ thể; 'ever' trong câu hỏi, 'never' = chưa bao giờ.
examples: Have you ever been to Singapore?

points.title: Present perfect vs past simple
points.structure_hint: have + past participle (no time) vs verb-ed (finished time)
points.explanation: Use past simple with a finished time (yesterday, last week). Use present perfect when the time is not stated.
points.vi_note: Quá khứ đơn khi có mốc thời gian đã xong; hiện tại hoàn thành khi không nói rõ thời gian.
examples: She played last week, and she has played many times.

points.title: First conditional (if + present, will)
points.structure_hint: If + present simple, ... will + base verb
points.explanation: Use the first conditional for a real future possibility and its result.
points.vi_note: Câu điều kiện loại 1 cho khả năng có thật trong tương lai và kết quả.
examples: If I train hard, I will improve.
If it rains, we will stay home.
If you study, you will pass.

points.title: used to (past habits)
points.explanation: Use 'used to' for habits or states in the past that are no longer true.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'used to' cho thói quen/trạng thái trong quá khứ nay không còn.
examples: I used to play every day.

points.title: Relative clauses (who / which / that)
points.explanation: Use 'who' for people, 'which' for things, and 'that' for both, to add information about a noun.
points.vi_note: 'who' cho người, 'which' cho vật, 'that' cho cả hai, để bổ sung thông tin cho danh từ.
examples: The player who scored is famous.
The phone which I bought is fast.
The game that we watched was close.

points.title: Gerund vs infinitive
points.explanation: Some verbs are followed by -ing (enjoy, finish) and some by 'to + verb' (want, decide, hope).
points.vi_note: Một số động từ theo sau bởi -ing (enjoy, finish), một số bởi 'to + verb' (want, decide).
examples: She decided to start the gym.

points.title: Second conditional (if + past, would)
points.structure_hint: If + past simple, ... would + base verb
points.explanation: Use the second conditional for imaginary or unlikely situations now or in the future.
points.vi_note: Câu điều kiện loại 2 cho tình huống tưởng tượng hoặc khó xảy ra ở hiện tại/tương lai.
examples: If I had more time, I would train every day.
If I were you, I would rest.
What would you do if you won?

points.title: Past continuous (was/were + -ing)
points.explanation: Use the past continuous for an action that was in progress at a moment in the past.
points.vi_note: Quá khứ tiếp diễn cho hành động đang diễn ra tại một thời điểm trong quá khứ.
examples: I was watching the match at 8.
They were playing when it rained.

points.title: Past continuous vs past simple (when / while)
points.explanation: Use the past continuous for a longer background action and the past simple for a short action that interrupts it.
points.vi_note: Quá khứ tiếp diễn cho hành động nền dài; quá khứ đơn cho hành động ngắn xen vào.
examples: I was sleeping when you called.
While she was cooking, the phone rang.
We were playing when it started to rain.

points.title: Present perfect continuous (have been + -ing)
points.explanation: Use it for an action that started in the past and is still continuing, often with 'for' or 'since'.
points.vi_note: Hiện tại hoàn thành tiếp diễn cho hành động bắt đầu trong quá khứ và còn tiếp tục, thường với 'for'/'since'.
examples: I have been training for two hours.
She has been studying since morning.
They have been playing all day.

points.title: for / since (with present perfect)
points.structure_hint: for + period of time; since + point in time
points.explanation: Use 'for' with a length of time and 'since' with a starting point.
points.vi_note: 'for' với khoảng thời gian, 'since' với mốc thời gian bắt đầu.
examples: I have lived here for three years.
She has been here since Monday.
We have known each other for a long time.

points.title: too / enough
points.structure_hint: too + adjective; adjective + enough; enough + noun
points.explanation: 'too' means more than needed (a problem); 'enough' means the right amount.
points.vi_note: 'too' = quá mức (gây vấn đề); 'enough' = đủ.

points.title: so / such
points.explanation: Use 'so' before an adjective and 'such' before a noun phrase, for emphasis.
points.vi_note: Dùng 'so' trước tính từ, 'such' trước cụm danh từ, để nhấn mạnh.
examples: It was such a good match.

points.title: Question tags
points.structure_hint: positive statement + negative tag; negative statement + positive tag
points.explanation: Add a short tag to check or confirm. A positive sentence takes a negative tag, and a negative sentence takes a positive tag.
points.vi_note: Thêm câu hỏi đuôi để xác nhận; câu khẳng định dùng đuôi phủ định và ngược lại.

points.title: Verb + object + infinitive (want/tell/ask someone to do)
points.explanation: Some verbs take an object then 'to + verb': want, tell, ask, would like, expect.
points.vi_note: Một số động từ + tân ngữ + 'to + verb': want, tell, ask, would like.
examples: I want you to help me.

points.title: Reported speech — statements
points.structure_hint: said (that) + clause (tense often shifts one step back)
points.explanation: To report what someone said, use 'said (that)' and often move the tense one step back.
points.vi_note: Để tường thuật lời nói, dùng 'said (that)'; thì thường lùi một bước.
examples: They said they liked the game.

points.title: Reported speech — questions
points.structure_hint: asked if/whether + clause; asked + question word + clause (statement word order)
points.explanation: Report yes/no questions with 'if/whether' and wh-questions with the question word, using statement word order.
points.vi_note: Tường thuật câu hỏi yes/no với 'if/whether', câu hỏi wh- với từ hỏi; dùng trật tự câu kể.
examples: She asked if I was ready.
They asked what time it started.

points.title: Relative clauses — whose / where / non-defining
points.structure_hint: whose + noun; where + place; ..., which/who ... (extra info, with commas)
points.explanation: Use 'whose' for possession, 'where' for places, and commas around extra (non-essential) information.
points.vi_note: 'whose' chỉ sở hữu, 'where' chỉ nơi chốn; dùng dấu phẩy cho thông tin bổ sung không thiết yếu.
examples: The player whose boots are red scored.
The gym where I train is new.
My phone, which is new, is fast.

points.title: make / let / allow (permission & causatives)
points.structure_hint: make/let + object + base verb; allow + object + to + verb
points.explanation: 'make' = force and 'let' = permit (both + base verb); 'allow' = permit (+ to + verb).
points.vi_note: 'make' = bắt buộc, 'let' = cho phép (+ động từ nguyên mẫu); 'allow' = cho phép (+ to + verb).

points.title: Past perfect (had + past participle)
points.structure_hint: had + past participle (an earlier past action)
points.explanation: Use the past perfect for an action that happened before another action in the past.
points.vi_note: Quá khứ hoàn thành cho hành động xảy ra trước một hành động quá khứ khác.
examples: The match had finished before I arrived.
She had eaten when I called.

points.title: Third conditional (if + past perfect, would have)
points.structure_hint: If + past perfect, ... would have + past participle
points.explanation: Use the third conditional to imagine a different past — something that did not actually happen.
points.vi_note: Câu điều kiện loại 3 để tưởng tượng một quá khứ khác (điều đã không xảy ra).
examples: If I had trained, I would have won.
If she had known, she would have come.
We would have stayed if it hadn't rained.
frames: If I had ___, I would have ___.

points.title: Passive voice — present & past
points.structure_hint: am/is/are + past participle; was/were + past participle
points.explanation: Use the passive when the action matters more than who does it. Form: be + past participle.
points.vi_note: Dùng bị động khi hành động quan trọng hơn người thực hiện. Cấu trúc: be + quá khứ phân từ.
examples: These phones are made in China.

points.title: Passive voice — other tenses & the by-agent
points.structure_hint: has been / will be / is being + past participle (+ by + agent)
points.explanation: The passive works in other tenses too. Add 'by + agent' only when it is important.
points.vi_note: Bị động dùng được ở các thì khác; thêm 'by + người thực hiện' chỉ khi quan trọng.
examples: The goal was scored by the captain.
The work will be finished tomorrow.

points.title: Modals of deduction (must / might / can't be)
points.explanation: Use 'must' when you are sure, 'might/could' when it is possible, and 'can't' when it is impossible.
points.vi_note: 'must' = chắc chắn, 'might/could' = có thể, 'can't' = không thể, để suy đoán hiện tại.

points.title: Past modals (must have / should have / could have)
points.explanation: Use these to guess about the past or talk about things you didn't do: 'should have' (regret), 'must have' (certain), 'could have' (possible).
points.vi_note: Dùng để suy đoán quá khứ hoặc nói điều đã không làm: 'should have' (tiếc), 'must have' (chắc chắn), 'could have' (có thể).

points.title: wish / if only
points.structure_hint: wish + past simple (present regret); wish + past perfect (past regret)
points.explanation: Use 'wish + past' for an unreal present and 'wish + past perfect' for a regret about the past.
points.vi_note: 'wish + quá khứ' cho điều không thật ở hiện tại; 'wish + quá khứ hoàn thành' cho tiếc nuối quá khứ.
examples: I wish I had more time.
I wish I had studied harder.
I wish it would stop raining.

points.title: Future continuous & future perfect
points.structure_hint: will be + -ing; will have + past participle
points.explanation: 'will be + -ing' = in progress at a future time; 'will have + past participle' = finished by a future time.
points.vi_note: 'will be + -ing' = đang diễn ra ở tương lai; 'will have + V3' = hoàn thành trước một mốc tương lai.
examples: This time tomorrow I will be flying.
By June I will have finished.
frames: This time tomorrow I will be ___ing.

points.title: Gerund vs infinitive — meaning change
points.explanation: Some verbs change meaning: 'stop doing' (quit) vs 'stop to do' (pause in order to do); 'remember to do' (a duty) vs 'remember doing' (a memory).
points.vi_note: Một số động từ đổi nghĩa: 'stop doing' (ngừng hẳn) vs 'stop to do' (dừng để làm); 'remember to do' vs 'remember doing'.

points.title: Verb/adjective + preposition + -ing
points.structure_hint: good at / interested in / tired of + verb-ing
points.explanation: After a preposition, use the -ing form: 'good at playing', 'interested in learning'.
points.vi_note: Sau giới từ, dùng dạng -ing: 'good at playing', 'interested in learning'.
examples: I am good at playing football.
She is interested in learning English.

points.title: Linking words — contrast (although / despite / however)
points.structure_hint: although + clause; despite/in spite of + noun or -ing; however + new sentence
points.explanation: Show contrast: 'although' + a clause, 'despite/in spite of' + a noun or -ing, and 'however' to start a new sentence.
points.vi_note: Diễn tả tương phản: 'although' + mệnh đề, 'despite' + danh từ/-ing, 'however' bắt đầu câu mới.
examples: Although it was hard, I finished.
It was tiring. However, it was fun.

points.title: Linking words — addition, reason, result
points.structure_hint: in addition / moreover; because of / due to; therefore / as a result
points.explanation: Connect ideas formally: add (in addition, moreover), give reasons (because of, due to), and show results (therefore, as a result). Useful for writing.
points.vi_note: Nối ý trang trọng: thêm (in addition), lý do (because of, due to), kết quả (therefore, as a result). Hữu ích cho viết.
examples: The team trained hard. As a result, they improved.
He was late due to the traffic.

points.title: Reduced relative clauses
points.explanation: Shorten a relative clause by dropping 'who/which + be': 'the man who is standing' becomes 'the man standing'.
points.vi_note: Rút gọn mệnh đề quan hệ bằng cách bỏ 'who/which + be': 'the man who is standing' -> 'the man standing'.
examples: The player standing there is the captain.
The phone made in Korea is popular.
